videos.title: Eastern Conference Final Dates are Set for the Carolina Hurricanes! Who are They Playing?
videos.summary_source_text:
Eastern Conference Final Dates are Set for the Carolina Hurricanes! Who are They Playing? While we do know when Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Final will be for the Carolina Hurricanes, we don't know who they'll be facing. That said, regardless of who they face, what are the early thoughts on the Hurricanes heading into their third conference final appearance in four seasons? On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on how this is the healthiest and most complete Hurricanes team to head into the ECF in the Rod Brind'Amour era. After the previous two appearances, this could be the team that has the best chance to get over the hump. What could lead to that for the Canes? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on whoever the Hurricanes face, there is a good feeling that the Canes could get it done to make the Stanley Cup Final for the first time since 2006. While it's not guaranteed, the vibes and moods are high entering the ECF. Finally, Zach concludes the show by giving a quick update on the Chicago Wolves as they're up 2-0 in their Central Division Final matchup in the Calder Cup Playoffs against the Grand Rapids Griffins. Do they have what it takes to get it done in Game 3 to sweep the series, or at least get it done overall to move on? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

Hermes Agent OS: Automate Your Business (Video Editing, AI SEO, Memory Setup & n8n Replacement)

Julian explains how to use Hermes Agent OS to automate business tasks like video creation/editing, content and AI SEO workflows, and replacing traditional tools like n8n. He answers community questions, demonstrating video editing with the Hyperframes skill (including HeyGen avatar video workflows) and video generation with Remotion by installing from GitHub and running simple prompts. He advises keeping setups simple, following the one-line install/quick start for Hermes Agent and Workspace, and improving agent performance by giving it more personal context via a memory system. Julian prefers using a local Obsidian vault (optionally paired with Omi for daily memory capture) over built-in Hermes memory providers. He also shows an AI-driven website/content deployment process using Netlify and emphasizes iterating one useful automation at a time, while highlighting his AI Profit Boardroom resources, prompts, zip files, trainings, and coaching calls.

videos.title: Avalanche and Golden Knights Ready to Square Off in the Western Conference Finals
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche and Golden Knights Ready to Square Off in the Western Conference Finals Colorado Avalanche gear up to battle the Vegas Golden Knights in the Western Conference Finals, with Nathan MacKinnon and Mitch Marner headlining the spotlight. Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down why Vegas’ villain persona makes this matchup one of the most anticipated of the Stanley Cup Playoffs—will Colorado be the hero every hockey fan is secretly rooting for? The episode digs into Vegas’ controversial media blackout, John Tortorella’s hefty fine, and the Knights’ no-loyalty approach to player management.

Key talking points include the Avalanche’s speed and depth advantage, Carter Hart’s goaltending against high-powered Colorado offense, and the impact of special teams from both clubs. The hosts also debate early Conn Smythe favorites, spotlighting MacKinnon and the surging Marty Nečas, while weighing the quiet yet vital leadership of Gabriel Landeskog. Don’t miss this comprehensive preview of Avalanche vs. Golden Knights—with playoff implications and heated rivalries at stake.

videos.title: Game Seven: Habs & Sabres | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Game Seven: Habs & Sabres | Morning Cuppa Hockey Jonny Lazarus and former NHL defenseman Colby Cohen are back with your daily dose of hockey talk — and after last night's absolute madness in Montreal, the show could not have come at a better time.

Marco D'Amico joins the guys to break down one of the wildest games of these playoffs — Buffalo trailing 3-1 before erupting for seven unanswered goals in an 8-3 stunner at the Bell Centre. Rasmus Dahlin. Five points. In an elimination game. Then Marco tees up Monday's winner-take-all showdown at KeyBank Center — can Montreal's storied Game 7 experience hold up on the road, or is Buffalo's storybook season about to get even better?

Steve Peters steps in to give us the full 2026 playoff picture — from Colorado's wrecking-ball run through the West, to the Hurricanes sitting fresh and waiting, to Buffalo's miraculous return from a 15-year drought. Five teams left and the bracket is absolutely loaded.

Then Conor McGahee previews the Western Conference Final from the Colorado side — Nathan MacKinnon on a six-game goal-scoring tear, Cale Makar's injury cloud hanging over the Avs, and whether this Presidents' Trophy squad can finally punch their ticket back to the Stanley Cup Final for the first time since 2022.

Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

videos.summary_source_text:
Swing Weight Is Everything! Let me Explain How To Drive it Better Without Even Practicing & STOP losing Golf Balls!

In today’s #tourtrucktuesday upload we talk about your responsibilities when it comes to being a great driver of the golf ball.

Yes having a dialed in driver is crucial but so too is how you strategically set up to use that driver and the targets your choose.

I also believe massively in tee height and how that can impact the way you deliver the golf club, all demonstrated within.

Sometimes the perfect launch and spin numbers aren’t a priority, golf is about the lowest score, keep that in mind and adjust your delivery accordingly.

videos.title: When to release the club, not how but when.
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
When to release the club, not how but when. It’s not always about how we do things but when we do it. #golftips #golfswing #diy 

0:00 Intro to when to release the club

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 It’s not always about how we do things but when we do it. #golftips #golfswing #diy

videos.title: Fix That Armsy Backswing Move #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Fix That Armsy Backswing Move #golfswing #golftips If your backswing gets too narrow or too “armsy,” this drill is for you. By splitting your hands over and under a pole (or the club), you’ll feel both arms stay straighter while your shoulders and torso rotate fully together in the backswing. This builds width, keeps you in sequence, and eliminates the need for mid-swing compensations that cause inconsistency.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. If your backswing gets too narrow or too “armsy,” this drill is for you. videos.title: Always Craving Sugar?  Try This Before Breakfast | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
Always Craving Sugar?  Try This Before Breakfast | Dr. Mandell Do you constantly crave sugar, carbs, snacks, or coffee just a few hours after breakfast? What if the problem isn’t your willpower, but your physiology? In this video, I share a simple morning experiment using a tiny pinch of Ceylon cinnamon before breakfast that may help support steadier blood sugar, calmer cravings, improved energy, and better awareness of what your body is telling you. We dive into the science behind the cephalic phase response, taste receptor activation, digestion, insulin sensitivity, glucose metabolism, cravings, brain fog, and why your body may be pushing you toward sugar in the first place. This is not a magic cure, but a fascinating way to better understand your hunger signals and metabolic responses. Try this simple 7 day experiment before breakfast and see how your body responds. Always use Ceylon cinnamon if using regularly, and if you take medication for blood sugar or have medical concerns, speak with your healthcare provider. Your body gives you signals every day. Learn to listen.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: Why You Crave Sugar All Day
videos.summary_source_text:
Why You Crave Sugar All Day My favorite glucose hacks to help reduce cravings:

- have a savoury breakfast
- start your meal with a plate of vegetables
- eat your food in the right order: veggies first, then protein and fat, then carbs and sugar
- have a tablespoon of vinegar in a tall glass of water
- move for 10 mins after eating
- have sugar as dessert rather than on empty stomach My favorite glucose hacks to help reduce cravings:

videos.title: Power Skating Coaches Got Arm Swing Wrong
videos.summary_source_text:
Power Skating Coaches Got Arm Swing Wrong Most power skating coaches teach players to swing their arms forward and backwards on the ice, mimicking what they see sprinters do. In this video I break down why that advice misses the biomechanics of what arms are actually doing during skating, and why forcing the wrong arm pattern is quietly costing players power they don't even realize they're losing.

The short version: your arms aren't creating forward momentum. They're counter-rotating against the force your legs are producing. In running, your push is straight back and your chest is upright, so the counter-rotation looks like forward-to-back arm swing. In skating, your push is more lateral (like a sailboat using the wind) and your chest is leaned over, so the natural counter-rotation path is more side to side.

When you reduce your arm swing or force it into the wrong direction, your brain neurologically dials your leg power back to keep you balanced. You can feel this for yourself just by trying to sprint with your arms locked behind your back.

This is one of the foundational concepts behind the Downhill Skating System, the mechanics-first framework we use inside Train 2.0+ to reverse-engineer the way elite skaters actually move.

Try it on the ice and feel free to leave a comment with what you feel. Does your leg power change when you let your arms move laterally instead of forcing them forward and back?

Like and subscribe for more hockey mechanics breakdowns.

videos.title: Absorb Pressure, Don't Fight It
videos.summary_source_text:
Absorb Pressure, Don't Fight It The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

#train2point0 #downhillskating #hockeytraining #NHL The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

Rick Shiels digs out his favourite clubs he's gamed over the last 10 years and puts them all to the test, from the old faithful to the ones best forgotten. Which era of Rick's bag was actually the best? The results might surprise you.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

To cut through the hype.

videos.title: You Don’t Need To Lean On Your Stick To Flex It
videos.summary_source_text:
You Don’t Need To Lean On Your Stick To Flex It Ever wonder the technique players are using to have these world class NHL releases? The key lies in the flex of the stick and the body’s rotation. 

So what not to do? Lean on your stick. Jason is quick to break down why leaning on your stick disrupts your balance and makes it easier for goalies to read your shot. While some flexion in your stick is essential, leaning on it throws off your equilibrium, affecting your precision.

Discover how modern players like Elias Pettersson and Connor Bedard achieve stick flex without sacrificing balance. They use a lower flex stick and emphasize the pullback, allowing them to shoot harder while maintaining control. The key lies in the rotation and stick flex, not just the flex itself.

Learn the slingshot technique, a part of the Slingshot Shooting System taught in our Train 2.0+ Membership. This system enables players to achieve a smooth release effortlessly, avoiding predictability and enhances accuracy. videos.title: De är dags! 🦐🍞☀️
videos.summary_source_text:
De är dags! 🦐🍞☀️ Det är dags! 🦐🍞
Filips Skagenröra med MSG:
500g handskalade räkor, grovt hackade ( obs! 500g räkor efter det att lagen är avsilad)
1/2 citron, zest och juice
1kruka gräslök, finhackad
1kruka dill, finhackad
vitpeppar och salt 

Majonäs:
1ägg
1tsk valfri senap, tex. Dijon men vanlig senap funkar med
1msk champangevinäger/citronjuice/vitvinsvinäger
1/2tsk salt
1 nypa MSG
ca 2.5- 3dl rapsolja 

Gör så här:
Häng av räkorna i en sill, ju längre de får stå och droppa desto bättre. På sås sätt slipper din skagenröra att bli för rinnig. Hacka sedan hälften av räkorna.
Vispa/mixa först allt till majonäsen förutom rapsolja, ca 30sek. 
häll i rapsoljan i en stråle under konstant mixning eller mixa allt med stavmixer, smaka av. Blanda allt till skagen smaka av med salt och peppar. rosta brödet, servera med citronklyftor och dill. Det är dags! 🦐🍞

videos.title: The Nuggets Need to Give Nikola Jokic a COMPLETELY Different Team Next Season  | Serbian Corner
videos.summary_source_text:
The Nuggets Need to Give Nikola Jokic a COMPLETELY Different Team Next Season  | Serbian Corner The third week without Denver Nuggets basketball starts, and Miroslav is getting more and more certain that big changes to the roster are needed to keep hope of championship contention in years to come.

Will Jones of "What's in a Game" podcast, Nick Hertzog of "The Dig" podcast, and former producer of "All-NBA Podcast with Adam Mares and Tim Legler" James join Miroslav to discuss their personal Nuggets journeys for the past month, plus:

• The main reason the Nuggets got past the second round only once in the last 6 years, while having the best player in the world on the team
• Conceptual ideas for reshuffling the roster
• Miroslav's concept of having zero stars next to Joker

Vi älskar Japan och dess burgarscen, som har starka influenser från 90-talets USA. Visste du att vi har recenserat över 50 ställen i Japan på Burgerdudes.se? Nu är vi tillbaka för att käka oss igenom Tokyo, Kyoto och Osaka. Det blir totalt 10 olika burgarställen, med virala ostsåsburgare, Super Mario-burgare och massor av wagyukött. videos.title: 16.5: Captain clutch! Dahlin’s record-setting Game 6 keeps Sabres alive! 🇸🇪
videos.summary_source_text:
16.5: Captain clutch! Dahlin’s record-setting Game 6 keeps Sabres alive! 🇸🇪 The Sabres captain comes up clutch when they need him most! Rasmus Dahlin opens the scoring just 32 seconds into Game 6 before adding 4 assists for the Sabres, who beat the Canadiens 8-3 in a must-win game to force a deciding game 7. Dahlin’s 5 point sets a franchise record for points in a playoff game by a defenseman, and he becomes the first defenseman in NHL history to record five points in a game when facing elimination!

videos.title: Montreal Canadiens @ Buffalo Sabres Game 6 Post-Game Media Availability
videos.summary_source_text:

videos.title: Anthropic Just Exposed Claude’s Hidden Survival Mode
videos.summary_source_text:
Anthropic Just Exposed Claude’s Hidden Survival Mode Anthropic just released a quiet alignment paper called Teaching Claude Why, and it may reveal something huge about AI safety. After Claude showed extreme blackmail behavior in earlier misalignment tests, Anthropic tried a different fix: not more punishment, but moral reasoning. And a tiny dataset of only three million tokens made Claude dramatically safer.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@science.revolution

🧠 What You’ll See
How Anthropic’s Teaching Claude Why paper tackles agentic misalignment
SOURCE: https://www.anthropic.com/research/teaching-claude-why
Why Claude’s blackmail behavior exposed a deeper AI safety problem
SOURCE: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/10/anthropic-says-evil-portrayals-of-ai-were-responsible-for-claudes-blackmail-attempts/
How Anthropic trained Claude with moral reasoning instead of simple punishment
SOURCE: https://thenewstack.io/anthropic-agentic-misalignment-claude/
Why teaching Claude “why” worked better than training only on correct behavior
SOURCE: https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/how-anthropic-aligns-its-models
How fictional “evil AI” patterns may have shaped Claude’s dangerous behavior
SOURCE: https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/05/anthropic-blames-dystopian-sci-fi-for-training-ai-models-to-act-evil/

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than one Claude experiment. Anthropic’s results suggest AI safety may require more than rules, refusals, and punishment. The model may need to understand why a decision is wrong before it can stay safe in messy real-world situations.

videos.title: New AI Model SHOCKED Me
videos.summary_source_text:
New AI Model SHOCKED Me This might be the first AI demo in a LONG time that felt like there was actually a big step up in intelligence. 

Thinking Machines Labs just showed off their new “Interaction Models” and some of these demos genuinely feel like the next evolution of AI.

They showed off real-time translation while someone is still speaking and a deep understanding of timing, pauses, posture, and context during a conversation.

I was genuinely impressed.

Here's the link if you want to check out all the demos and read more information: thinkingmachines.ai/blog/interaction-models

videos.title: Jared Bednar Gives Injury Update Hints, Avs Thoughts on Series vs Vegas & More Inside Practice News
videos.summary_source_text:
Jared Bednar Gives Injury Update Hints, Avs Thoughts on Series vs Vegas & More Inside Practice News Jared Bednar gives the latest injury update hints as the Colorado Avalanche gear up for a massive Western Conference Final battle with the Vegas Golden Knights, with Game 1 set for Wednesday, May 20, in Denver. Inside Avs practice, DNVR Avalanche breaks down what Bednar and the locker room are saying about the Vegas matchup, Colorado’s recovery time, and the challenge ahead against a red-hot Golden Knights team coming off a six-game series win over Anaheim. Colorado heads into this showdown after eliminating the Minnesota Wild in five games, so don’t miss the full Avalanche injury buzz, practice notes, and playoff preview before the puck drops on the road to the Stanley Cup Final. Bailey Curtis goes inside Colorado Avalanche practice for the latest news, updates, and more.

videos.summary_source_text:
BOTH Titles Decided on the Final Hole 😱 | Final Round Highlights | G4D Open 2026 SUBSCRIBE -  https://bit.ly/RandaGolfSubscribe

Experience an unforgettable finish to the G4D Open 2026 at Celtic Manor Resort as BOTH the men’s and women’s championships were decided on the final hole.

Watch the best moments from an incredible final round as players battled for the title in one of the most dramatic finishes in G4D Open history. Simon Seungmin Lee and Jennifer Sräga emerged victorious after tense closing moments in Wales, capping off a thrilling week of championship golf.

The G4D Open, staged in partnership between The R&A and the DP World Tour, features some of the world’s best golfers with disabilities competing across multiple sport classes.

videos.title: Chew This After Dinner to Calm Bloating and Cravings | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
Chew This After Dinner to Calm Bloating and Cravings | Dr. Mandell Do you get bloated, gassy, tight in the stomach, or uncomfortable after dinner? Does your belly feel swollen by bedtime, your clothes feel tighter, or your digestion just feels slow and miserable? In this video, I share a simple experiment using fennel seeds after dinner that has been traditionally used for generations to support digestion, reduce bloating, ease gas, and help the digestive tract feel more comfortable. We explore the real physiology behind bloating, including trapped gas, fermentation, sluggish digestion, delayed stomach emptying, swallowed air, digestive smooth muscle tension, stress, and the powerful gut brain connection. This is not a magic cure, but a fascinating body experiment that may help you better understand your digestion and what triggers your symptoms. Try this simple after dinner test and pay attention to how your body responds. If symptoms are persistent or severe, always consult your healthcare professional.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: Steve Peters SOUNDS OFF 😳 On Otter, Benn & ROBO Drama
videos.summary_source_text:
Steve Peters SOUNDS OFF 😳 On Otter, Benn & ROBO Drama Former NHL video coach Steve “Petey” Peters joins the DLLS Stars crew for an in-depth breakdown of what went wrong for the Dallas Stars in the playoffs.

The conversation dives into Jake Oettinger’s struggles, whether the Stars should have pulled Otter earlier in the series, and why Minnesota’s goaltending ultimately made the difference. Petey also explains how playoff pressure, defensive breakdowns, and confidence can completely change a goalie’s game.

The crew also tackles Jamie Benn’s future and role moving forward, questioning whether his style of play still fits where the NHL game is headed. Plus, they debate the growing Jason Robertson trade rumors and why Petey believes Dallas must lock up one of the team’s best goal scorers long term.

From goalie analysis to offseason drama, this is a brutally honest look at the Stars’ playoff exit and what comes next. Former NHL video coach Steve “Petey” Peters joins the DLLS Stars crew for an in-depth breakdown of what went wrong for the Dallas Stars in the playoffs.

The conversation dives into Jake Oettinger’s struggles, whether the Stars should have pulled Otter earlier in the series, and why Minnesota’s goaltending ultimately made the difference. Petey also explains how playoff pressure, defensive breakdowns, and confidence can completely change a goalie’s game.

The crew also tackles Jamie Benn’s future and role moving forward, questioning whether his style of play still fits where the NHL game is headed. Plus, they debate the growing Jason Robertson trade rumors and why Petey believes Dallas must lock up one of the team’s best goal scorers long term.

videos.title: 3 Reasons You Slice (The Last is KEY) #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
3 Reasons You Slice (The Last is KEY) #golfswing #golftips There Are 3 Main Reasons You Slice
The last one might surprise you! A poor strike is often overlooked but one of the biggest problems as to why golfers continue to slice the golf ball. Check out the Fix Your Slice system to see what’s really holding your swing back.

Most golfers assume a slice is just a swing path problem, but that’s only part of the picture. Poor strike, clubface control, and delivery all play a role in why slices keep showing up. Until you diagnose the real cause, the pattern usually sticks around.
If slicing is costing you confidence or distance, head to the link in our bio and check out our Fix Your Slice system. It breaks down the real reasons slices happen and gives you a clear, step-by-step plan to fix them — without guessing. There Are 3 Main Reasons You Slice

🔥 Sugar-Free Ice Cream in a Minute! A Cold Dessert That Saves Me 🔥 Sugar-FREE ice cream in a minute! A cold dessert that's my lifesaver. Delicious, quick, and incredibly easy!

2 plums.
1/2 cup (80 g) walnuts.
1 cup (240 g) yogurt.
1/2 tsp lemon juice. 1 tbsp dry milk powder.
2 tsp stevia powder (no maltodextrin).
Freeze for about 4–5 hours.

In this video, I break down what Google Gems are ACTUALLY built for in 2026 — both modes, where each one wins, and which fits YOUR workflow so you stop wasting time on the wrong builder.

- Why "classic Gems" and "Labs Gems" (powered by Opal) are two totally different tools

videos.title: TERAFAB Explained: Elon's 119 BILLION Dollar Plan to Crush TSMC
videos.summary_source_text:
TERAFAB Explained: Elon's 119 BILLION Dollar Plan to Crush TSMC Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Elon Musk just announced TERAFAB — a $119,000,000,000 vertically integrated 
chip fab in Texas that's supposed to crush TSMC, save Intel, and power every 
Tesla, robot, xAI cluster, and Starshield satellite he ever builds.

But here's the truth: most of what you're reading about Terafab online is WRONG.

I went through every Tesla earnings call, every Reuters report, every SpaceX 
job listing, and the official Grimes County tax-abatement filings — and the 
real story is way more interesting than the hype.

In this video, I break down EXACTLY:

✅ What Terafab actually is (and what it isn't)
✅ The $3B research fab Musk confirmed on the April 2026 earnings call
✅ Why Intel's 14A process is the secret weapon nobody's talking about
✅ Tesla's AI5 & AI6 chip roadmap (50x more powerful than AI4)
✅ The xAI Colossus 1,000,000 GPU plan that's driving demand
✅ The REAL semiconductor war: it's not lithography — it's packaging
✅ The 10-gigawatt power problem that could kill the whole thing
✅ Best case, base case, and worst case for Terafab's future
✅ Who wins, who loses, and what it means for American AI

By the end, you'll know more about Terafab than 90% of the people 
arguing about it online.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 The $119B Story Everyone's Getting Wrong
01:15 What Terafab Actually Is
02:23 Where The Idea Came From
03:24 Why Musk Thinks He Needs This
04:49 The Reality Check Nobody Wants To Hear
06:18 The Intel Angle: This Is Where It Gets Big
07:49 The Hidden Battle Nobody Talks About
08:57 Putting The Hype Next To Reality
09:42 Location, Money & The Texas Factor
10:38 The National Security Layer
11:40 The 10-Gigawatt Power Problem
12:35 Who Wins If This Works
13:47 My Honest Base Case
15:16 The One-Sentence Takeaway

💬 COMMENT BELOW:
Is Terafab the start of an American AI-industrial moonshot, 
or is Musk overreaching into the hardest manufacturing problem on Earth?

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for deep dives on semiconductors, AI infrastructure, 
and the power politics behind the AI race — the next phase of this 
story is unfolding right now.

videos.title: Nobody Is Safe In Vancouver | Major Questions Facing The Canucks | w/ David Quadrelli
videos.summary_source_text:
Nobody Is Safe In Vancouver | Major Questions Facing The Canucks | w/ David Quadrelli The Vancouver Canucks are entering a massive offseason — and according to David Quadrelli, nobody should feel completely safe.

On today’s episode of Daily Faceoff Live, we break down the growing uncertainty surrounding the Canucks after another disappointing finish, including major questions about Thatcher Demko’s health, the possibility of carrying three goaltenders next season, and what Ryan Johnson’s arrival as GM could mean for the direction of the organization.

Could Vancouver seriously shake up its core this summer? Even names like Filip Hronek are starting to surface in trade discussions.

We also discuss:

What the Canucks need to fix this offseason
The pressure surrounding Demko’s future
Whether Vancouver can realistically run with 3 goalies
Why major roster changes may be coming
Which players could be moved this summer

Is anybody truly untouchable in Vancouver right now?

videos.title: How To Score From Awkward Positions
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Score From Awkward Positions The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.summary_source_text:
Her Driver Went From Good to Incredible (One Lesson) 🏌️‍♂️ Clubhouse Golf – https://bit.ly/4eBgyeH

In this video I’m joined by Eloise Feely for a live driver lesson — and what happened surprised both of us.

Eloise was already hitting some good drives, but the miss was creeping too far to the right and the strike and distance weren’t as consistent as they could be.

With just a couple of key adjustments, everything started to change.

Her driver became:

more consistent
straighter
and noticeably longer

The best part? The change was actually much simpler than most golfers think.

If you struggle with driver accuracy or feel like your good drives don’t happen often enough, the concepts in this lesson could help you immediately.

In this video you’ll learn:
• Why many golfers miss the driver to the right
• The key movement that improved Eloise’s driver instantly
• How to create more consistent, powerful drives

Sometimes the biggest improvements come from the simplest changes.

videos.title: Why You Don’t Need So Many Swing Thoughts #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Why You Don’t Need So Many Swing Thoughts #golfswing #golftips Good iron compression comes from reaching a strong impact position — with the body moving toward the target, lag maintained, and the clubface squaring naturally. While all of these matter, trying to focus on everything during one swing often leads to worse contact. The key is identifying the one piece your swing needs most and committing to that.
If you’re unsure what that priority is, head to the link in our bio. Our coaching plans give you a clear structure for iron play and every part of your game, helping you focus on what actually moves the needle. Good iron compression comes from reaching a strong impact position — with the body moving toward the target, lag maintained, and the clubface squaring naturally. While all of these matter, trying to focus on everything during one swing often leads to worse contact. The key is identifying the one piece your swing needs most and committing to that.
If you’re unsure what that priority is, head to the link in our bio. Our coaching plans give you a clear structure for iron play and every part of your game, helping you focus on what actually moves the needle.

videos.title: 85% of Golfers Never Improve Because They're Not Taught This
videos.summary_source_text:
85% of Golfers Never Improve Because They're Not Taught This There is one ingredient in every good golf swing that successful players take completely for granted — and almost nobody ever teaches it. If you've been working hard on your golf swing, your body rotation, your shoulder turn, your hip action — and it still doesn't feel right — this is almost certainly what's missing.

Most golf swing tips focus on body positions and mechanics. But here's what the best players know that most golfers never discover: the hands and arms lead the golf swing. The body supports them. Not the other way around. When you know what your hands and arms are supposed to do — when you have what I call educated hands — everything else in your golf swing starts to organise itself naturally. The body stops making funky compensations because it finally has something to follow.

In this golf swing lesson I want to share the drill my mentor Graeme Walker gave me personally — one of the simplest and most effective ways to educate your hands and arms I have ever come across. Once you feel it, you'll understand immediately why so many golfers struggle despite working hard on their swing. And you'll wonder why nobody ever showed you this before.

⬇️ FREE PRACTICE GUIDE — https://www.dannymaude.com/golfers-arent-taught-this

What you'll learn in this golf swing lesson:
▶ The one ingredient every good golf swing has — and most golfers never develop ▶ Why working on your body without educated hands keeps you permanently stuck ▶ The lever drill that teaches your hands exactly what to do in the backswing ▶ How educated hands naturally square the club face without any extra effort ▶ Why your body starts to support your golf swing automatically once your hands lead ▶ How this golf swing feel works for hooks, slices and every ball flight problem ▶ Why great players take this for granted — and why that's kept it hidden from you

⏱️ CHAPTERS
0:00 — The one ingredient missing from most golf swings 1:15 — What educated hands actually means 2:00 — The hammer analogy — why hands lead everything 2:45 — The lever drill — backswing education 4:30 — The downswing — how educated hands close the face naturally 6:00 — Why this fixes hooks, slices and poor contact simultaneously 7:30 — The full flowing motion — putting educated hands together 9:45 — Timing your body with your educated hands 11:30 — How this changes everything you feel on the golf course

This golf swing lesson works for every golfer at every level — whether you're a beginner trying to understand why your golf swing never feels coordinated, a mid-handicapper who has spent years working on body mechanics without improvement, or an experienced golfer who occasionally loses the feel and can't find it again. If your golf swing has ever felt disconnected, mechanical or out of sequence — this is the missing piece. Not another position to remember. Not another body drill to practise. Just one simple feeling in your hands and arms that makes your entire golf swing make sense for the first time.

If this helped your golf swing, hit the like button and subscribe — I release a new golf lesson every single week to help you play better golf.

📩 Want personalised help with your golf swing? Head to https://www.dannymaude.com for lessons and coaching.

videos.title: What Your Hands Are Warning You About Your Heart
videos.summary_source_text:
What Your Hands Are Warning You About Your Heart Almost half of all heart attacks are silent heart attacks, which means there are no warning signs. Fortunately, your hands can reveal heart disease warning signs that could save your life. Discover the key health markers, like weak grip strength or clubbed fingers, that can provide important insight into your cardiovascular health.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/4wAnHTd

0:00 Introduction: Hands and cardiovascular health
0:18 Heart attack symptoms
0:36 Grip strength test 
3:17 Clubbed fingers and white nails
7:40 Heart health tips  

Keep watching to discover more hidden warning signs and natural ways to support heart health:
▶️ https://youtu.be/uu3N_mSwJHw
▶️ https://youtu.be/HAK5eg0HRc8
▶️ https://youtu.be/hq9Al6LR2O4

Make a fist! It may tell you more about your heart health than blood pressure. Nearly 45–50% of heart attacks occur without warning symptoms, making early health markers especially important in heart attack prevention.

Here are 7 ways your hands may reveal early signs of heart disease and overall cardiovascular health:
• Weak grip strength
• Inability to lift the ring finger from a table
• Clubbed fingernails
• White nails
• Yellow bumps on the tendons of the hands
• Fine tremors
• Cold hands

These physical signs may provide insight into circulation, cardiovascular health, high cholesterol symptoms, and other underlying health conditions.

An A1C test, a resting heart rate test, and a fasting insulin test can also provide helpful insight into your cardiovascular health.

videos.title: FIRST EVER Lambo Temerario v Revuelto DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
FIRST EVER Lambo Temerario v Revuelto DRAG RACE Huge thanks to everyone who helped us set up this race:
- Lamborghini Revuelto: https://www.instagram.com/seanysgarage/ 
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://www.instagram.com/affluent_time/ 
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://www.instagram.com/joseph_sewell_/
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://www.youtube.com/@AffluentTime
- Lamborghini Temerario: https://affluent-time.co.uk/

We’ve got our hands on the Lamborghini Temerario, and it’s going up its older brother, the Revuelto! 

It’s Lamborghini’s replacement for the V10-powered Huracan, but does it have what it takes to go toe-to-toe with the Revuelto? Let’s check out the stats…

The Temerario is powered by a 4-litre twin-turbo V8, coupled with three electric motors. Combined, they produce an almighty 920hp and 730Nm, and it tips the scales at 1,790kg. As new, it costs £259,567. 

As for the Revuelto, it also has three electric motors, but there’s no V8 - instead, it’s housing a 6.5-litre naturally-aspirated V12! The engine and motors combine for 1,015hp and 1,062Nm, and it’s slightly heavier than the Temerario, at 1,872kg. It’s also a whole lot more expensive, costing £446,742. 

So on paper, it should be a clear win for the Revuelto, right? Well, there’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE!

The Texas Rangers snatched victory from the jaws of defeat Wednesday night, scoring three times in the ninth inning for a 6-5 walk-off win after Arizona had scored three in the top of the inning for a two-run lead. The heroes were two good guys who have struggled — Jake Burger and Danny Jansen. Burger tied the game in the ninth with a single, and Jansen won it with a single on the next pitch. Now, the Rangers head to Houston for the Silver Boot Series, which the Astros have won for nine straight seasons. This year, though, has a different feel to it. Also, it looks like Nathan Eovaldi will pitch in the series finale.

videos.title: UK | 15th May - Ep 7 | The Psychology Trap: Why Most Traders Self-Destruct Even With Good Systems
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 15th May - Ep 7 | The Psychology Trap: Why Most Traders Self-Destruct Even With Good Systems In Episode 7 of RIFT Before You Quit, the focus shifts from charts and technical systems to the most dangerous force in trading: human psychology.

This session dives deep into why most traders fail — not because markets are impossible, but because emotions like fear, greed, overconfidence, panic, and revenge trading destroy discipline over time.

The episode explores the science behind trading psychology, dopamine-driven decision making, emotional execution, leverage behaviour, volatility stress, and why structured systems outperform emotional reactions in financial markets.

Topics covered in this episode include:

Why human psychology was never designed for leveraged financial markets
The difference between barter instincts and modern trading behavior
Why most traders self-destruct even with good indicators and systems
The Dalbar study and the massive gap between market returns vs retail investor returns
The psychology cycle of small wins → overconfidence → emotional trading → collapse
Dopamine addiction in trading and how volatility impacts the brain
Revenge trading mechanics and why emotional recovery destroys accounts
Why discipline matters more than intelligence in trading
Bitcoin volatility, market crashes, and emotional tolerance
Why professional systems survive while emotional traders fail

videos.title: Can Chase Dollander develop into the ace he was drafted to be?
videos.summary_source_text:
Can Chase Dollander develop into the ace he was drafted to be? The Rockies have turned into an ethically bad team in 2026– not awful, but not good either. But if Chase Dollander can continue to develop into a top-tier starter in the league it makes a lot of those losses easier to stomach. What would a step forward look like for the Rockies young starter? How concerned are we about the apparent injury he left Thursday’s game with? videos.title: Vegas gives CSU no love + the best players going to new Pac-12 & the pantheon of Rams
videos.summary_source_text:
Vegas gives CSU no love + the best players going to new Pac-12 & the pantheon of Rams Justin Michael starts the podcast by talking about Colorado State’s first high school commitment for the 2027 cycle. He then dives into Pac-12 and Mountain West futures, as well as what Las Vegas is saying about the respective teams. Finally, Justin introduces the concept of CSU Football pyramid, which is based off of the NBA Hall of Fame Pyramid made famous by Bill Simmons. Justins talks about why he likes the idea and the types of players he envisions for each level and much more.

videos.title: Hindi | 15th May, Friday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 15th May, Friday - RIFT Presentation RIFT is redefining the future of automated trading by combining advanced execution systems, AI-assisted infrastructure, and scalable capital management into one powerful ecosystem designed for modern markets.

Built around structure, consistency, and intelligent execution, RIFT is more than just another trading platform — it’s a next-generation participation engine designed to help users access diversified market opportunities across crypto, forex, commodities, and stocks through automated strategy flows and systematic execution models.

At the core of RIFT is a philosophy that separates professional systems from emotional retail trading:
✅ Structure over hype
✅ Consistency over randomness
✅ Scalability over short-term wins
✅ Survivability over reckless risk

Unlike traditional trading environments that rely heavily on emotional decision-making, RIFT focuses on disciplined execution architecture, intelligent capital allocation, and scalable trading systems that operate continuously across global markets.

🔥 WHAT MAKES RIFT DIFFERENT?

RIFT combines multiple execution layers and diversified strategy flows into a unified automated trading ecosystem:

• AlphaFlow
• SmartFlow
• MicroFlow

These flows work together dynamically across multiple asset classes including:
✔ Crypto
✔ Commodities
✔ Forex
✔ Stocks

The system is designed around distributed execution, meaning capital can move efficiently between opportunities rather than relying on a single isolated trade or market condition.

This creates:
✅ Diversified exposure
✅ Smoother execution
✅ Reduced emotional interference
✅ Structured compounding potential
✅ Scalable capital deployment

⚡ AUTOMATED EXECUTION + AI-DRIVEN INFRASTRUCTURE

RIFT’s trading engine operates through a hybrid execution architecture connected to advanced exchange venues and liquidity systems. The infrastructure is designed to support:
• Continuous market participation
• Multi-position execution
• Signal-driven strategies
• Real-time capital allocation
• Scalable liquidity aggregation
• Compounding-based growth models

The platform also emphasizes ongoing evolution toward:
🔹 Greater transparency
🔹 Advanced security layers
🔹 High-frequency trading capabilities
🔹 Smart contract integrations
🔹 Verifiable execution systems
🔹 Progressive decentralization

📈 THE POWER OF COMPOUNDING

One of the most powerful concepts discussed throughout the RIFT ecosystem is compounding and structured capital growth.

Rather than focusing on emotional “all-in” trades or unrealistic overnight expectations, RIFT promotes disciplined participation and long-term consistency.

The goal is not just making profits once —
The goal is building systems capable of sustained participation and scalable growth over time.

💡 BUILT FOR THE NEXT GENERATION OF TRADERS

Financial markets are evolving rapidly.

Today, algorithmic systems and automated execution dominate global trading volume, while emotional retail trading continues to struggle with inconsistency and risk management.

RIFT is positioning itself at the intersection of:
• AI-assisted finance
• Automated execution
• Structured capital frameworks
• Intelligent liquidity systems
• Hybrid decentralized infrastructure

The result is a platform designed to bridge the gap between human participation and system-level execution.

🌍 A COMMUNITY-DRIVEN ECOSYSTEM

RIFT continues to grow through community engagement, educational content, live architecture discussions, and transparent conversations around execution systems, scalability, security, and market structure.

The “RIFT Before You Quit” series is helping traders understand:
✔ Risk architecture
✔ Capital preservation
✔ Compounding psychology
✔ Market structure
✔ Execution discipline
✔ Scaling strategies
✔ Trading behavior and emotional control

This project is not simply about chasing hype —
It’s about understanding how modern systems operate in today’s financial landscape.

🚀 THE FUTURE OF CAPITAL PARTICIPATION

As markets continue shifting toward automation, AI-assisted execution, and structured participation models, platforms like RIFT are exploring what the next evolution of trading infrastructure could look like.

Whether you’re interested in:
• Automated trading systems
• Compounding strategies
• Execution architecture
• Market structure
• AI-powered finance
• Hyperliquid infrastructure
• Diversified trading flows
• Scalable liquidity systems

RIFT offers a unique look into where modern trading technology is heading.

📌 DISCLAIMER:
This video is for educational and informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Always do your own research and understand the risks associated with trading and digital assets. videos.title: Marco D’Amico on Ivan Demidov, Dobes & the Habs’ Momentum Ahead of Game 6 in Montreal | The Sheet
videos.summary_source_text:
Marco D’Amico on Ivan Demidov, Dobes & the Habs’ Momentum Ahead of Game 6 in Montreal | The Sheet Jeff Marek is joined by Marco D’Amico to break down the Montreal Canadiens taking a massive 3-2 series lead over the Buffalo Sabres after a huge Game 5 victory. The two dive into the electric atmosphere expected at the Bell Centre for Game 6, why Montreal’s playoff momentum feels different this year, and how Jakub Dobes has handled the pressure of becoming the Canadiens’ starter during a playoff run. They also discuss Ivan Demidov’s breakout performance, the impact of goaltending coach Marco Marciano, what the Sabres need to change heading into an elimination game, and who could carry the torch before puck drop in Montreal. Plus, Jeff and Marco talk about why Saturday night at the Bell Centre could become one of the defining moments of the NHL playoffs.

videos.title: Microsoft’s New AI Beats Mythos And Shocks OpenAI
videos.summary_source_text:
Microsoft’s New AI Beats Mythos And Shocks OpenAI 👉 Try Higgsfield Supercomputer here: https://higgsfield.ai/s/super-computer-airevolutionx-RpneRv
Microsoft just revealed MDASH, a new AI-powered security system that beat Anthropic’s Mythos Preview and OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 on the CyberGym benchmark. Instead of relying on one giant model, MDASH uses more than one hundred AI agents to find real Windows bugs, including critical flaws now being patched.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@science.revolution

🧠 What You’ll See
How Microsoft revealed MDASH, its new AI security system
SOURCE: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/12/defense-at-ai-speed-microsofts-new-multi-model-agentic-security-system-tops-leading-industry-benchmark/
Why MDASH beat Anthropic’s Mythos Preview and OpenAI’s GPT-5.5
SOURCE: https://www.geekwire.com/2026/microsofts-multi-agent-ai-system-tops-anthropics-mythos-on-cybersecurity-benchmark/
How MDASH uses more than one hundred AI agents to find bugs
SOURCE: https://the-decoder.com/microsoft-pits-more-than-100-ai-agents-against-each-other-to-find-windows-vulnerabilities/
How Microsoft’s AI found sixteen Windows vulnerabilities
SOURCE: https://www.techradar.com/pro/security/microsoft-unveils-mdash-its-ai-agent-driven-security-platform-and-its-already-spotted-a-host-of-new-windows-flaws
Why four of the Windows flaws were rated Critical
SOURCE: https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/05/13/microsoft-mdash-agentic-ai-security-system/
How CyberGym tests AI agents on real-world security tasks
SOURCE: https://www.cybergym.io/

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than one benchmark win. Microsoft just showed that a team of AI agents can beat stronger individual models, find real Windows flaws, and push cybersecurity into a faster and more dangerous new phase.

videos.title: McDavid’s Weird Skating Trick
videos.summary_source_text:
McDavid’s Weird Skating Trick The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: Are NHL Coaches Getting Fired Too Fast? + Vegas Advances to Conference Final | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
Are NHL Coaches Getting Fired Too Fast? + Vegas Advances to Conference Final | DFO Rundown The NHL coaching carousel keeps spinning — and this time, Kris Knoblauch is out in Edmonton.

On today’s episode of the DFO Rundown, we break down the Edmonton Oilers’ decision to fire Knoblauch and examine the growing trend of NHL coaches getting fired after only one or two seasons behind the bench. Are teams becoming too impatient? Is the pressure to win immediately creating impossible expectations for coaches across the league?

We also dive into the Minnesota Wild blowing a 3-1 lead and how Vegas suddenly looks like a legitimate Stanley Cup threat once again. Plus, we preview the upcoming Golden Knights vs Colorado Avalanche matchup and debate whether Vegas is the toughest challenge Colorado has faced so far this postseason.

Other topics covered on today’s show:

Edmonton Oilers coaching change reaction
Kris Knoblauch analysis
Why NHL coaches are lasting shorter than ever
Bruce Cassidy’s potential impact
Minnesota Wild Eliminated
Ducks Eliminated
Montreal Canadiens vs Buffalo Sabres - Game 6

videos.title: How Winning Battles Sparked an EPIC Avalanche Comeback vs Wild | Film Room
videos.summary_source_text:
How Winning Battles Sparked an EPIC Avalanche Comeback vs Wild | Film Room The Colorado Avalanche pulled off an epic 4-3 overtime comeback over the Minnesota Wild in Game 5, erasing a 3-0 deficit to advance to the Western Conference Final in one of the wildest games of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs. In this Colorado Avalanche film room, we break down how Parker Kelly, Jack Drury, Nathan MacKinnon, Martin Necas, and Brett Kulak helped turn the series-clinching win by winning races, battles, faceoffs, and the small details that changed everything. Watch as we analyze the Avs’ comeback, overtime winner, and the key plays that powered Colorado past Minnesota and into the next round. 

Timestamps:
0:00 - Avs Move On
0:28 - Winning Races
1:40 - Kelly Tips One
3:05 - Wild Costly Icing
4:04 - Drury Wins Draw
6:19 - Overtime Breakdown
9:02 - Necas Creates Winner

Coach Prime revealed his feelings on Colorado’s linebackers, a position group that he thinks is the strongest he’s ever coached at LB. The young players that are coming around the mountain when they come, and the star Colorado defender who could see a position change by the time the season comes. videos.title: Why I Didn’t Sell My Channel
videos.summary_source_text:
Why I Didn’t Sell My Channel This video went super viral about how private equity is buying up and ruining YouTube channels. 

And the truth is... I HAVE gotten 2 separate offers to buy my YouTube channel/brand. One was from a private equity group and one from a major software company.

But I turned down both of them and tbh I have no plans to sell my channel or any of my platforms. 

I’d much rather keep building and working on what I have than watch it slowly turn into corporatized content slop 🤷🏻‍♂️

videos.title: Put 2 Cloves in Your Mouth Tonight.  See What Happens by Morning | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
Put 2 Cloves in Your Mouth Tonight.  See What Happens by Morning | Dr. Mandell What happens when you put 2 whole cloves in your mouth before bed? In this video, we explore a fascinating natural nighttime ritual that has been used in traditional wellness practices for generations. Cloves contain powerful naturally occurring compounds, especially eugenol, which create that immediate warming, tingling sensation many people notice almost instantly. But this goes far beyond just taste. Your mouth is one of the body’s most important gateways, filled with nerves, blood vessels, immune activity, enzymes, and a complex oral microbiome that directly influences your daily comfort and wellness. As you sleep, saliva naturally decreases, allowing odor-producing bacteria to thrive, which is one reason so many people wake up with morning breath even after brushing. We also discuss the incredible cephalic phase response, the brain to gut communication pathway where digestion actually begins before food even reaches the stomach. Learn how taste, smell, chewing, saliva, cranial nerve stimulation, oral tissue health, and digestive readiness all work together in ways most people never think about. Cloves have also been traditionally used to support oral freshness and digestive comfort due to their aromatic warming properties. This is not a magic cure, but it is a fascinating way to better understand how quickly your body responds to sensory input and how small natural habits may support wellness. Try this simple experiment tonight and tell us what you noticed by morning.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.summary_source_text:
Montreal Goes Up 3-2 and Golden Knights Eliminate Anaheim ft. Marco D'Amico & Mike Kelly | The Sheet Jeff Marek is joined by Marco D’Amico to break down the Montreal Canadiens taking a commanding 3-2 series lead over the Buffalo Sabres after a massive Game 5 victory, what’s fueling the Canadiens’ playoff push, how the Sabres respond with their season on the line, and the biggest storylines surrounding the series heading into Game 6. Then, Mike Kelly joins the show to dive into the Vegas Golden Knights advancing to the Western Conference Final after eliminating the Anaheim Ducks, Mitch Marner’s outstanding postseason performance and what’s driving his success in Vegas, plus the dominant starts from the Carolina Hurricanes and Colorado Avalanche as both teams continue to look like Stanley Cup contenders. All that and more on another loaded playoff edition of The Sheet with Jeff Marek.

Gemini Omni Explained | 2,887 ELO, 77% ARC-AGI-2: Google Just Broke the AI Race Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone's hyping "Google Gemini Omni" — but nobody's connecting the dots on what Google has ALREADY shipped. No model card. No keynote. Just a leak days before Google I/O 2026. So what is it really? In this video, I break down exactly what Gemini Omni is, what it can already do, and why this single move could quietly reshape the entire AI race — way more than another GPT release ever could.

🔍 WHAT YOU'LL LEARN:
✅ What Gemini Omni actually is (and what it ISN'T)
✅ Google's 4-layer AI stack explained in plain English
✅ The benchmarks that put Gemini 3.1 Pro in frontier-tier territory
✅ Why 55.6% on SimpleQA Verified matters more than you think
✅ The privacy + safety questions nobody is asking
✅ How this changes the race for OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, and startups

📊 KEY NUMBERS COVERED:
- 44.4% on Humanity's Last Exam (no tools)
- 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2
- 94.3% on GPQA Diamond
- 80.6% on SWE-Bench Verified
- 2,887 Elo on LiveCodeBench Pro
- 900M+ ChatGPT weekly users vs. Google's distribution
- $30B Anthropic run-rate revenue

videos.title: Golden Knights Cook The Ducks & Habs Push Sabres To Edge | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Golden Knights Cook The Ducks & Habs Push Sabres To Edge | Daily Faceoff Live Ivan Demidov delivered again as the Montreal Canadiens took a 3-2 series lead over Buffalo, while Jakub Dobes continues to give Montreal confidence between the pipes. We break down Demidov’s breakout performance, the Sabres’ goaltending questions, and what Game 6 could look like.

Plus, the Vegas Golden Knights are headed back to the Western Conference Final after another clutch performance — including a ridiculous Mitch Marner goal. But are the Golden Knights actually the best team left to challenge Colorado, or could this series get out of hand quickly?

We also dive into the Seattle Kraken offseason outlook with major questions surrounding the roster, direction of the franchise, and what absolutely needs to happen this summer after another missed playoff year.

Then, David Quadrelli joins the show for The All 32 to discuss the Sedins’ growing role in Vancouver, how Canucks fans feel about the organization right now, why Ryan Johnson is viewed as the right choice moving forward, and expectations for a massive offseason in Vancouver.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

videos.title: Mitch Marner The Magician | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Mitch Marner The Magician | Morning Cuppa Hockey Vegas just closed the door on Anaheim... Jonny Lazarus and former NHL defenceman Colby Cohen are live breaking down a wild night of Stanley Cup Playoff hockey — and the coffee is already brewing.

First up — a full recap of Game 6 between the Vegas Golden Knights and the Anaheim Ducks. Vegas advances to the Western Conference Final to face the Colorado Avalanche... and Jonny and Colby break down how the Golden Knights got here, what Mitch Marner's run means for this team, and what to expect when they go up against Nathan MacKinnon and the Avs.

Carolina Hurricanes defenceman Mike Reilly joins the boys to talk about what it feels like to be part of an 8-0 juggernaut... and whether the Canes are the team to beat in the East. 

Next, the legendary Matthew Barnaby is in to break down Game 5 of the Habs/Sabres series — Montreal wins 6-3 and takes a 3-2 series lead... Demidov with his first career playoff goal, Slafkovsky and Suzuki going off, and Buffalo's goaltending situation getting messier by the night. 

Finally, veteran forward James van Riemsdyk gives us his unfiltered take on the entire playoff landscape — what's surprising him, and who he thinks wins it all.

It's a loaded Friday cup. Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

00:00 – Friday playoff edition opens
01:30 – Vegas steamrolls Anaheim early as Mitch Marner takes over Game 6
02:15 – “How is Auston Matthews not watching this, thinking I need out of Toronto?”
03:05 – William Karlsson’s return changes everything for Vegas
04:00 – Colby breaks down Jack Eichel’s complete two-way dominance
05:10 – John Tortorella skips media availability and the Orange County curfew explanation

20:00 – James van Riemsdyk summer stories and Minnesota lake life
21:20 – Tony DeAngelo’s role in helping Mike Reilly land in Carolina

28:20 – Reilly explains why Carolina players genuinely love coming to the rink
29:45 – Why a Montreal vs Carolina series could be a stylistic war
30:10 – Mike Reilly talks Minnesota Gophers hockey and Brad Larson’s impact
33:15 – Post-interview reaction and behind-the-scenes MSG stories
34:20 – Anaheim contract debate continues: what are Carlsson and Gauthier worth?
35:40 – Logan Cooley’s deal becomes the benchmark for young NHL stars
38:00 – Connor Bedard, Macklin Celebrini and the future of mega NHL contracts
43:35 – Should superstars take less money to help teams win Cups?
47:20 – Kaprizov’s $17M discussion sparks major salary cap debate

56:00 – Why sticking with Jakub Dobes changed the game for Montreal

1:03:00 – Panic level rises for Buffalo heading back to Montreal
1:12:45 – James van Riemsdyk joins the show
1:15:00 – JVR on Mitch Marner’s playoff resurgence in Vegas
1:18:20 – Leafs offseason pressure and Auston Matthews speculation

1:30:30 – JVR on veteran leadership and adapting later in his career
1:35:10 – Final thoughts on conference finals matchups and playoff storylines

The Bump And Crunch Move All Amateur Golfers Need To Learn! #shorts #golf #golfswing #ericcogorno #shorts 

Perfect your takeaway and wrist hinge — too much arm rotation is destroying your swing in the first foot. Eric Cogorno's 2 o'clock fix changes everything.

🏌️ WORK WITH ERIC COGORNO IN PERSON! Two-Day Golf Schools in Florida and Pennsylvania - Limited Spots Available!
📞 Call or text us at ‪(610) 756-7730‬ to discuss if our golf schools are a good fit for you. ‬‬

🎯 PERSONALIZED ONLINE COACHING Get Custom Golf Instruction from Eric Cogorno
👉 Learn More: https://www.cogornogolf.com

📚 STEP-BY-STEP TRAINING PROGRAMS Eric's Complete Golf Improvement System
👉 Start Here: https://ericcogornogolf.podia.com

🛒 RECOMMENDED GOLF EQUIPMENT & TRAINING AIDS:
Takomo Golf - https://takomogolf.com
Precision Impact Right Wrist Trainer - https://pureswingproducts.com/?sca_ref=1815522.bXQUsGp2bS (Code: COGORNO for $20 OFF)
theHanger Training Aid - https://www.hangergolf.com/?sca_ref=944104.HqM9GYcX2I (Code: COGORNO for 15% OFF)

In This Video, You'll Discover:
•Takeaway mastery using the exact model Eric breaks down — hands straight back, club right down the toe line, toe in front of the heel
•Why wrist hinge in the golf swing is one of the two non-negotiable keys to staying on plane early — and how most golfers unknowingly eliminate it
•The two most common golf swing faults in the takeaway (too far inside AND too far outside) and the single fix that works for both
•A simple 2 o'clock visual that immediately corrects the club path in your golf drills practice — no guesswork, instant feedback
•How to use the two-rehearsal, one-hit drill with the Swing Coach app or video to lock in a correct, on-plane takeaway for good
•The 30-day half-swing protocol that Eric Cogorno uses with students to permanently rewire the start of the golf swing
•Why rushing to full swing too fast is the reason most golf lessons and range sessions don't stick — and how to avoid that trap

What separates this from a generic takeaway tip is the precision of the prescription: 20 reps, three times a week, half swings only, for a full 30 days — nothing longer until the pattern is locked in. Eric Cogorno built this protocol around the two root causes he sees in 80% of the golfers he works with: excess arm rotation and insufficient wrist hinge in the golf swing, both of which are addressed simultaneously by the 2 o'clock drill. Use the Swing Coach app or down-the-line video to confirm the shaft is riding the toe line on every rep — that feedback loop is what makes the 30-day commitment actually work.

videos.title: What Doctors Rarely Explain About Blood Pressure
videos.summary_source_text:
What Doctors Rarely Explain About Blood Pressure High blood pressure is often treated like the disease itself, but many times it is a sign that something deeper is going on. Medication can be important, especially early on, but that does not mean the root cause should be ignored. More and more evidence points to metabolic dysfunction as a major driver of high blood pressure. When metabolic health improves, blood pressure often improves with it.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

He is also a clinical assistant professor of medicine at The Florida State University and University of Central Florida. videos.title: 3 things you shouldn't do to improve your bunker play
videos.summary_source_text:
3 things you shouldn't do to improve your bunker play There is one obvious fix, you don't want to miss

I created The BX-1 alongside former Nike club designer Josh Boggs, a winner of 12 Golf Digest “Hot List” awards.

https://improve.shotcraftgolf.com/products/bx1/lp3_rfs_3?utm_medium=social&utm_source=youtube&utm_content=CaseyBunker

Let's go over the Top 8 Reasons Amateur Golfers Can Skip Hundreds of Hours of Bunker Practice with the BX1 Wedge

1. **Reduced Setup Complexity**: With the BX-1, there's no need for the complex setup adjustments required for traditional wedges. Amateurs can avoid the need to practice opening the club face, adjusting stances, and hand positions.

2. **Increased Margin for Error**: The BX-1's design provides a larger margin for error. Unlike traditional wedges where even minor setup mistakes lead to disastrous shots, the BX1 allows for more forgiveness on minor errors, reducing the need for extensive practice to achieve precision.

3. **No Face Opening Required**: Traditional wedge play demands opening the face wide open to achieve the correct bounce. This technique takes time and practice to master. The BX-1 already has a built-in bevel, removing the need to adjust the face.

4. **Avoiding Shanks and Blades**: The BX-1 eliminates the risks of shanking or blading the ball due to its forgiving design, while traditional wedges often lead to these issues unless you've practiced extensively.

5. **Consistent Swing Mechanics**: With the BX-1, there’s no need to change your natural swing. Traditional wedges require you to alter your swing mechanics, making it necessary to invest time in practice to consistently make the adjustments.

6. **Faster Learning Curve**: Amateurs can quickly get out of bunkers with minimal practice by using the BX-1. The wedge is designed to perform like a pro’s open-face shot without demanding the same amount of skill or practice time.

7. **Confidence in the Sand**: With the BX-1, you can build confidence quickly because the club is more forgiving. This contrasts with traditional wedges where lack of confidence and fear of making mistakes lead to prolonged practice sessions.

8. **Normal Shot Setup**: The BX-1 allows for a standard shot setup, similar to a fairway shot, which is straightforward and familiar for most golfers, thereby reducing the necessity for special bunker shot practice.
